tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and acidic pH, suggesting impaired renal function, likely acute or chronic kidney disease with possible tubular dysfunction. Hemoglobin A1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. Urine is cloudy and brown, but no evidence of infection or hematuria. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for further assessment and management.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: Azienda Sanitaria Locale n° 7 Musei
